behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: source adapters -> aggregation/composition -> summary input payload. A unit test of one function can miss wiring bugs (e.g., one adapter omitted, wrong field mapping). Real OS/network is not required because source clients can be in-process fakes returning deterministic payloads. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about inclusion in the handoff payload, not real HTTP behavior or model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector/orchestration modules with fake clients for metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log. Execute one report build and assert the exact object/string passed into summary generation contains all three source sections with expected key fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (collector failure -> orchestrator continues -> report section annotation). Unit is too low because the failure appears at handoff between retrieval, orchestration, and formatting modules. Real network/process is unnecessary; an in-process fake source raising timeout/exception reproduces the failure mode. Rejected higher levels: text does not require real email/network transport or LLM output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with two fake sources returning data and one fake source raising an availability error. Assert run completes, output includes a `data unavailable` marker for that source section, and no global abort is raised.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model judgment/generation quality (faithfulness to input values). A stubbed summary generator would always pass and hide hallucination/omission/regression failures. This is a single agentic decision point, not a multi-agent chain. Rejected Integration: behavior text explicitly evaluates semantic accuracy of generated narrative, which deterministic fixtures cannot validate.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative weekly datasets to the real summary model/prompt path and score outputs against faithfulness checks (all critical numeric values preserved, no contradictory claims, tolerable paraphrase). Run multiple trials and fail on systematic misrepresentation beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real infrastructure: networked data sources/email provider and actual inbox delivery semantics. Mocks can hide SMTP/API auth, transport, MIME formatting, and deliverability issues. Rejected Agentic: for this behavior, pass/fail is delivery to inbox, not narrative quality; summary content can be fixture-controlled while validating end-to-end transport path.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script end-to-end in a test environment using reachable staging endpoints and a real mailbox. Verify one scheduled run produces an email in the configured inbox with expected subject/body attachment structure and source-derived sections present.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is local orchestration/state handoff across runs: send failure -> durable local save -> next invocation loads pending report -> retry. This is best caught by wiring modules with real local filesystem and a fake failing/recovering mail client. Real external network and cron are not strictly required to reproduce logic defects. Rejected System: behavior text does not require validating actual scheduler daemon or real provider outage mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Run invocation 1 with mail client stubbed as unreachable; assert report file is persisted in retry store. Run invocation 2 with mail client stubbed as reachable; assert pending file is consumed and send attempted exactly once, then retry artifact is cleared.
